headline: Sabah Healthcare Access Gap Kills 340 Annually
context: Sabah Health Department data shows 340 preventable deaths in 2025 attributed to delayed medical access in remote districts.

cards.big: 340 preventable deaths in Sabah linked to healthcare access gaps in 2025
cards.sub: Patients in 14 remote districts average 4.5 hours travel to nearest hospital

cards.big: Sabah has 1 doctor per 2,800 people versus 1 per 850 in Kuala Lumpur
cards.sub: Rural clinics in Tongod and Pitas operate with a single medical officer each

cards.big: Flying Doctor Service covers only 120 of 380 identified remote communities
cards.sub: Budget cuts reduced helicopter medical evacuations by 30% from 2023 to 2025

cards.big: 8 district hospitals lack functioning operating theatres as of March 2026
cards.sub: Surgical patients must be transferred to Queen Elizabeth Hospital in KK

cards.big: Preventable deaths measure whether healthcare is a right or a postcode lottery
340 preventable deaths yearly — Sabah's healthcare gap is a resource allocation failure, not scarcity.
